outbound.connection_note: YVR has US Customs Preclearance - clear customs in Vancouver, arrive Tokyo as domestic. Connection is 1h 32m - move quickly.

baggage.notes: 1 checked bag + 1 cabin bag + 1 backpack per person included. Second checked bag $100 per trip.

hotels.name: JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
hotels.notes: 5 min walk from Shinjuku Station South Exit
hotels.checkin_note: Only hotel with 2 PM check-in — arrives May 12 after long flight, perfect

hotels.name: Fujiya Hotel (est. 1878)
hotels.onsen_description: Historic mineral pools - indoor and outdoor rotenburo
hotels.notes: Japan's oldest Western hotel. Yukata and geta provided. 3 min walk from Miyanoshita Station.
hotels.checkin_note: Send bags via TA-Q-BIN May 15 morning

hotels.name: Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
hotels.notes: 5 min walk to Hanamikoji Street. Free late-night ramen 9:30-11 PM nightly.

hotels.name: Dormy Inn Premium Namba ANNEX
hotels.onsen_description: Rooftop natural hot spring with city views
hotels.notes: Lawson attached. Free late-night ramen 9:30-11 PM. Rooftop onsen open til midnight.

hotels.name: Residence Hotel Takayama Station
hotels.notes: 30 seconds from Takayama Station. Full apartment with kitchen.
hotels.checkin_note: ⚠️ Front desk only 6:30 AM–midnight — not 24hr. May 23 early checkout: key drop box available.

hotels.name: Yuen Shinjuku
hotels.onsen_description: Rooftop onsen - indoor and outdoor with Tokyo skyline views
hotels.notes: 8 min walk from Shinjuku Station. Yukata provided.
hotels.checkin_note: ⚠️ Early check-in explicitly NOT offered. Arriving ~9:40 PM May 23 — no issue. If arriving early, store bags at front desk.

onsens.type: Historic mineral pools - indoor + outdoor rotenburo
onsens.notes: First onsen of the trip! Historic mineral baths since 1878.
Dawn rotenburo - almost empty at this hour.

onsens.type: Last mountain mineral bath before Kyoto
Natural hot spring with city views
onsens.notes: Follow up with free late-night ramen downstairs!

onsens.type: Indoor + outdoor with Tokyo skyline views
onsens.notes: Best onsen views of the trip. Shinjuku skyline at night.

trains.notes: Activate JR Pass at Narita JR counter BEFORE boarding.

trains.seat_tip: Seat 1A or 1B for panoramic front view. RIGHT side for Mt Fuji at 40 min mark.
RIGHT side D seats - Mt Fuji visible ~10:20 AM between Odawara and Shin-Fuji. LAST MT FUJI VIEW OF TRIP.
trains.book_at: JR office at Odawara Station on the morning
trains.notes: Fully reclining seats, meal + drinks, hot towel, amenity kit. Only 18 seats per train - reserve early!
Transfer at Nagoya for Wide View Hida.
trains.supplement_note: Green Car upgrade - reserve at Shin-Osaka or Osaka Station JR Midori-no-madoguchi

trains.seat_tip: LEFT side A/B seats - Hida River gorge appears ~90 min in.
LEFT side A/B seats - gorge views on return too.

trains.jr_pass_warning: NOT covered by JR Pass - buy separately at Kintetsu office east side Nagoya Station. ~Y1,720 each way. Buy BOTH directions at same time.
trains.notes: Then 20-min Mie Kotsu shuttle bus from Shiroko to circuit. Suica accepted.

trains.seat_tip: Any reserved seat - evening train, Fuji view unlikely after dark.

trains.notes: Depart 14:20 sharp. Return Suica card at Narita JR office for balance + Y500 deposit refund.

itinerary.label: Pre-Departure Night

schedule.activity: Final pack - both checked bags (1 per person)
schedule.notes: Max 23kg / 50lb each. Lay everything out first then pack.

schedule.activity: Final pack - both cabin bags (1 per person)
schedule.notes: Max 10kg. Must fit overhead: 56x36x23 cm.

schedule.activity: Final pack - both backpacks (personal items)
schedule.notes: Under-seat item. Pack tomorrow's snacks + entertainment.

schedule.activity: Weigh ALL bags
schedule.notes: Checked max 23kg, cabin max 10kg. Overweight fees are expensive.

schedule.activity: JR Pass vouchers x2 in carry-on - NEVER checked bag
schedule.notes: Lose these and the trip gets very expensive very fast.

schedule.activity: Both passports in carry-on
schedule.notes: Double check validity - must be valid 6+ months past May 2026.

schedule.activity: Download all booking confirmations offline on phone
schedule.notes: teamLab, Sumo, Romance Car, Tea Ceremony, Suzuka, Air Canada.

schedule.activity: Charge all devices + power banks fully overnight
schedule.notes: Phones, camera, earbuds, power banks - all plugged in.

schedule.activity: Download Japan offline maps in Google Maps (on home WiFi)
schedule.notes: Do this on WiFi - large download.

schedule.activity: Download Japanese language pack in Google Translate (on home WiFi)
schedule.notes: Camera translate is essential for menus and signs.

schedule.activity: Lock all doors and windows
schedule.notes: Front door, back door, garage, all windows.

schedule.activity: Turn off all lights - check every room
Turn off and unplug kitchen appliances
schedule.notes: Oven, toaster, coffee maker - unplug them all.

schedule.activity: Turn off and unplug non-essential electronics
schedule.notes: TVs, lamps, desktop chargers.

schedule.activity: Adjust thermostat for 2 weeks away
schedule.notes: May in Texas - set around 78F, no need to run AC hard.

schedule.activity: Empty fridge of anything that will go bad
Take out trash
Schedule Uber for 06:30 AM pickup tomorrow
schedule.notes: Do this in the Uber app tonight - scheduled rides are more reliable.

schedule.activity: Set TWO alarms - 05:45 AM and 06:00 AM
schedule.notes: Early flight - no snoozing.

tips: JR Pass vouchers must be in carry-on - losing them is catastrophic
Schedule Uber tonight rather than requesting in the morning
Leave nothing perishable in the fridge - 2 weeks is a long time

itinerary.label: Departure Day

schedule.activity: Wake up - both alarms
schedule.notes: Last morning at home for 2 weeks!

schedule.activity: Get dressed + final bathroom sweep
schedule.notes: Don't forget phone chargers, razors, last-minute toiletries.

schedule.activity: Final house walkthrough
schedule.notes: Lights off? Doors locked? Oven off? Fridge closed?

schedule.activity: Uber pickup - Cedar Park (Veterans Park area)
schedule.notes: 1 hour to Austin-Bergstrom Airport (AUS).

schedule.activity: Arrive Austin-Bergstrom Airport (AUS)
schedule.notes: Head to Air Canada check-in counter.

schedule.activity: Check in + bag drop - Air Canada counter
schedule.notes: 2 checked bags (1 per person, included). Counter closes ~07:50.

schedule.activity: Clear TSA security
schedule.notes: AUS is usually fast. Allow 30 min to be safe.

schedule.activity: At gate - grab coffee and last US breakfast
✈️ AC 1763 departs Austin (AUS)
schedule.notes: Boeing 737 MAX 8. 4h 38m. Air Canada Bistro meal available for purchase.

schedule.activity: Land Vancouver (YVR) Terminal M
schedule.notes: 1h 32m connection - move quickly off the plane.

schedule.activity: Clear US Customs Preclearance at YVR
schedule.notes: YVR processes US customs in Canada - you arrive in Tokyo as a domestic passenger. Follow signs.

schedule.activity: Walk to Air Canada international gate
schedule.notes: Terminal M - follow AC Japan signs. YVR is well signed.

schedule.activity: Board AC 3 - get settled on 787-9
schedule.notes: Much bigger plane - Dreamliner. Better air quality and humidity than older aircraft.

schedule.activity: ✈️ AC 3 departs Vancouver (YVR)
schedule.notes: Boeing 787-9. Wi-Fi available. Two full meals included. 9h 55m.

schedule.activity: Cross International Date Line - Monday becomes Tuesday
schedule.notes: You skip overnight - May 11 ends, May 12 begins while in the air.

tips: YVR connection (1h 32m): deplane fast, follow Air Canada Japan signs, clear customs, go straight to gate
Dreamliner 787-9 tip: window shades are electronic - dim but don't fully block for better sleep
Try to sleep on the Vancouver-Tokyo leg - arrive refreshed

itinerary.label: Arrive Tokyo

schedule.activity: 🛬 Land Tokyo Narita (NRT) Terminal 1
schedule.notes: Welcome to Japan! You cleared customs in Vancouver so you walk out as a domestic arrival.

schedule.activity: Immigration + baggage claim
schedule.notes: Faster than normal since customs was done at YVR. Allow 30-45 min.

schedule.activity: Activate JR Pass x2 at Narita JR ticket counter
schedule.notes: Ground floor Terminal 1. Show vouchers + passports. This is your most important task on arrival.

schedule.activity: Pick up 2x Suica cards at JR counter
schedule.notes: Load Y5,000 each (~$35). Covers all metro, buses, convenience stores.

schedule.activity: Board N'EX Narita Express to Shinjuku
schedule.notes: Platform B1. 90 min. Covered by JR Pass.

schedule.activity: Arrive Shinjuku Station - South Exit
schedule.notes: 5 min walk to hotel.

schedule.activity: Check in - JR Kyushu Hotel Blossom Shinjuku
schedule.notes: Night 1 of 4.

schedule.activity: Dinner - Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane)
schedule.notes: 2 min walk. Smoky yakitori alley. Very atmospheric first Tokyo meal.

schedule.activity: Kabukicho + Golden Gai evening stroll
schedule.notes: 8 min walk. 200 tiny bars. Each fits 6-8 people. Just walk in and sit.

schedule.activity: Back to hotel - early night
schedule.notes: Jet lag is real. Tomorrow starts at 10 AM.

tips: Activate JR Pass FIRST before doing anything else at Narita
Golden Gai: cover charge ~$5-8, just pick any bar and sit down
You skipped a night flying so jet lag hits hard tonight - sleep early

itinerary.label: Kimono + Asakusa + teamLab

schedule.activity: Depart hotel
Kimono Rental Wargo - Asakusa
schedule.notes: Metro: Shinjuku > Ginza > Asakusa. Book at wargokyoto.com in advance.

schedule.activity: Senso-ji Temple + Nakamise shopping street
schedule.notes: 5 min walk from Wargo. Free entry.

schedule.activity: Tsukiji Outer Market - graze lunch
schedule.notes: Toei Line > Higashi-Ginza. Fresh tuna, uni, tamagoyaki.

schedule.activity: Meiji Jingu Shrine
schedule.notes: Hibiya Line > Harajuku. Forested approach path.

schedule.activity: Takeshita Street - kawaii fashion and crepes
schedule.notes: 3 min walk from shrine.

schedule.activity: Hachiko Statue + Shibuya Scramble Crossing
schedule.notes: 12 min walk.

schedule.activity: Starbucks 2nd floor - aerial view of the crossing
schedule.notes: Above Shibuya Crossing.

schedule.activity: Dinner - Ichiran Ramen Shibuya
schedule.notes: 5 min walk. Solo booth ramen experience.

schedule.activity: teamLab Planets Toyosu - immersive digital art
schedule.notes: Yamanote > Shimbashi > Yurikamome. Timed entry - book at teamlab.art!

schedule.activity: Return to Shinjuku
schedule.notes: Yurikamome > Yamanote.

tips: Wear slip-on shoes today - easy on/off for kimono fitting AND teamLab water rooms
teamLab sells out weeks ahead - must pre-book at teamlab.art

itinerary.label: Shibuya Sky + Sumo Natsu Basho

schedule.activity: Depart hotel
Shibuya Sky - observation deck floors 45-46
schedule.notes: JR Yamanote > Shibuya (7 min). Book at shibuya-scramble-square.com.

schedule.activity: 360 degree views + open air rooftop + Mt Fuji view
schedule.notes: Morning gives best Fuji visibility before haze builds.

schedule.activity: Travel to Ryogoku - sumo town
schedule.notes: JR Yamanote > Akihabara > JR Sobu. 35 min.

schedule.activity: Chanko nabe pre-sumo lunch at Tomoegata
schedule.notes: 2 min from Kokugikan. Wrestler's hot pot stew.

schedule.activity: Collect sumo tickets at will-call
schedule.notes: Pre-booked at sumo.or.jp.

schedule.activity: Enter Ryogoku Kokugikan - grab arena bento + beer
🏆 Sumo Natsu Basho - Makuuchi top division bouts
schedule.notes: May tournament runs May 11-25. Yokozuna bout ~17:45.

schedule.activity: Dinner - Chanko Dining Wakamatsu
schedule.notes: 5 min walk from arena.

schedule.activity: JR Sobu Line back to Shinjuku
schedule.notes: 30 min.

schedule.activity: Optional - Omoide Yokocho drinks
schedule.notes: 2 min from hotel.

tips: Book Shibuya Sky 10:30 AM slot - best Mt Fuji visibility before haze
Arriving at sumo at 2 PM gives full lower + top division experience

itinerary.label: Romance Car + Open Air Museum + Fujiya Hotel
itinerary.luggage_note: Send large bags via TA-Q-BIN to Fujiya Hotel this morning. Travel with daypack only.

schedule.activity: TA-Q-BIN bags forwarded to Fujiya Hotel
schedule.notes: JR Hotel front desk. Bags arrive Fujiya afternoon.

schedule.activity: Check out JR Hotel - daypack only
Odakyu Romance Car - Shinjuku > Hakone-Yumoto
schedule.notes: Book at odakyu.jp at least 2 weeks ahead. 85 min scenic mountain ride.

schedule.activity: Arrive Hakone-Yumoto - buy 2-day Hakone Free Pass
schedule.notes: Station kiosk. Covers ALL Hakone transport.

schedule.activity: Ashiyu free foot baths at Hayakawa riverside
schedule.notes: 3 min walk from station. Free.

schedule.activity: Hakone Tozan Railway > Miyanoshita
schedule.notes: 15 min switchback mountain railway.

schedule.activity: Drop bags at Fujiya Hotel front desk
schedule.notes: 3 min walk from Miyanoshita Station.

schedule.activity: Lunch - Fujiya Hotel Orchid Room
schedule.notes: Historic chandeliers and mountain views.

schedule.activity: Tozan Railway > Hakone Open Air Museum
schedule.notes: 10 min.

schedule.activity: Hakone Open Air Museum - sculptures + Picasso + foot onsen
schedule.notes: Don't miss the foot onsen inside the museum!

schedule.activity: Proper check in - yukata and geta sandals provided!
schedule.notes: Japan's oldest Western hotel. Est. 1878.

schedule.activity: ♨️ ONSEN 1 - Fujiya Hotel historic mineral pools
schedule.notes: First onsen of the trip!

schedule.activity: Dinner - Fujiya Hotel Orchid Room
schedule.notes: Try Western course - served since 1878!

schedule.activity: ♨️ ONSEN 1 continued - second evening soak
schedule.notes: Hotel baths are quieter now.

tips: Tomorrow is the earliest start of the trip - set alarm for 6:15 AM tonight!
Open Air Museum foot onsen: soak while looking at sculptures and Hakone mountains

itinerary.label: Mt Fuji + Kawaguchiko + Owakudani
itinerary.early_start_reason: Mt Fuji clearest before 11 AM - today's early start is essential

schedule.activity: ♨️ ONSEN 2 - dawn rotenburo at Fujiya Hotel
schedule.notes: Almost empty at dawn. Mountain mineral water at sunrise.

schedule.activity: Quick breakfast - 7-Eleven or Fujiya Hotel
Tozan Railway > Hakone-Yumoto
schedule.notes: 15 min.

schedule.activity: Fujikyuko Highway Bus > Kawaguchiko
schedule.notes: 90 min ride. Book at highway-buses.jp.

schedule.activity: Buy Kawaguchiko Retro Bus Pass (1-day)
schedule.notes: Station kiosk.

schedule.activity: Climb 398 stone steps to Chureito Pagoda
schedule.notes: Red Line bus to Shimo-Yoshida then 5 min walk.

schedule.activity: 🗻 CHUREITO PAGODA - iconic Mt Fuji view
schedule.notes: Stand LEFT of pagoda for classic shot with Fuji centered behind all five stories.

schedule.activity: Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway (Kachi Kachi Yama)
schedule.notes: Red Line bus to ropeway base.

schedule.activity: Ring the love bell at the summit together
schedule.notes: Summit shrine - couples tradition!

schedule.activity: Lunch - Kawaguchiko lakefront hoto noodles
schedule.notes: 5 min walk from ropeway.

schedule.activity: Lake Kawaguchi stroll + optional swan boat
Fujikyuko Bus back to Hakone-Yumoto
schedule.notes: 90 min return.

schedule.activity: Hakone Shrine + Peace Torii Gate on Lake Ashi
schedule.notes: Bus to Hakone-machi.

schedule.activity: Lake Ashi rowboat - 30 min
schedule.notes: Lakeside dock near shrine.

schedule.activity: Pirate Ship - Hakone-machi to Togendai
schedule.notes: 30 min lake cruise. Free with Hakone Free Pass.

schedule.activity: Hakone Ropeway > Owakudani volcanic valley
schedule.notes: 15 min aerial cable car.

schedule.activity: 🌋 Owakudani - sulfur vents + kuro tamago black eggs
schedule.notes: Eating black eggs adds 7 years to your life!

schedule.activity: Ropeway > Cable Car > Railway back to Miyanoshita
schedule.notes: 40 min total.

schedule.activity: Late dinner - Fujiya Hotel
♨️ ONSEN 3 - Tenzan Tohji-kyo (or Fujiya Hotel soak)
schedule.notes: Tenzan = forest riverside onsen by taxi. Or use Fujiya Hotel baths if tired.

tips: Check fujisan.jp the night before for Mt Fuji visibility forecast
Chureito: stand LEFT of pagoda for the classic five-story + Fuji shot
Last ropeway down from Owakudani is ~9 PM - our 8:15 PM arrival gives 45 min

itinerary.label: Gran Class Shinkansen + Kyoto Arrival
itinerary.fuji_view_alert: Mt Fuji visible RIGHT side Gran Class (D seats) between Odawara and Shin-Fuji ~10:20 AM - LAST MT FUJI VIEW OF THE ENTIRE TRIP

schedule.activity: ♨️ ONSEN 4 - final dawn rotenburo at Fujiya Hotel
schedule.notes: Last Hakone onsen. Last mountain mineral water.

schedule.activity: Final Fujiya Hotel breakfast
schedule.notes: Orchid Room - savour it!

schedule.activity: Check out Fujiya Hotel
Tozan Railway > Hakone-Yumoto > Odawara
schedule.notes: 30 min total.

schedule.activity: Reserve Gran Class seats at JR office - Odawara Station
schedule.notes: Show JR Pass, pay Gran Class supplement. Only 18 seats per train - reserve ASAP!

schedule.activity: 🌟 GRAN CLASS Hikari Shinkansen - Odawara > Kyoto
schedule.notes: SIT RIGHT SIDE D SEATS! Fuji view ~10:20 AM.

schedule.activity: 🗻 MT FUJI - RIGHT SIDE - LAST FUJI VIEW OF THE TRIP!
schedule.notes: Between Odawara and Shin-Fuji stations. Camera ready!

schedule.activity: Gran Class service - meal + drinks + hot towel + amenity kit
schedule.notes: Fully reclining seats, cabin attendant. Enjoy every minute.

schedule.activity: Arrive Kyoto Station
schedule.notes: Welcome to Kyoto!

schedule.activity: Lunch - Kyoto Ramen Koji (10th floor of station)
schedule.notes: 8 different ramen restaurants on one floor.

schedule.activity: Taxi to Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
Check in Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
schedule.notes: Gion area. 5 min walk to Hanamikoji Street.

schedule.activity: Taxi to Higashiyama District
Kiyomizu-dera Temple
schedule.notes: 10 min uphill through Ninenzaka.

schedule.activity: Sannenzaka + Ninenzaka historic stone-paved lanes
schedule.notes: Downhill walk through machiya shops.

schedule.activity: Yasaka Shrine at dusk - stone lanterns glowing
Gion - Hanamikoji Street - spot maiko 18:30-19:30!
schedule.notes: Walk slowly, no flash photography, bow respectfully.

schedule.activity: Dinner in Gion area
Taxi back to hotel
schedule.notes: 10 min.

schedule.activity: ♨️ Dormy Inn Kyoto onsen

tips: Gran Class: only 18 seats per train - reserve at Odawara JR office first thing!
Fuji view: RIGHT side D seats, ~10:20 AM - this is the LAST Mt Fuji view of the trip
Maiko spotting: 18:00-19:30 on Hanamikoji - walk slowly, respect their space

itinerary.label: Arashiyama + Tea Ceremony + Samurai Museum

schedule.activity: Taxi to Kyoto Station
schedule.notes: 10 min.

schedule.activity: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
schedule.notes: JR Sagano Line > Saga-Arashiyama (25 min). Walk deeper than 100m!

schedule.activity: Tenryu-ji Zen Garden - UNESCO, designed 1339
schedule.notes: 3 min walk from grove.

schedule.activity: Lunch in Arashiyama
schedule.notes: Shigetsu vegetarian or Yoshimura soba.

schedule.activity: Oi River walk + Togetsukyo Bridge
JR Sagano > Kyoto Station > Karasuma Subway
🍵 Camellia Tea Ceremony - 90 min authentic
schedule.notes: 8 min walk from Karasuma-Oike. Book at camellia-teaceremony.com!

schedule.activity: Nijo Castle - nightingale floors! UNESCO Heritage
schedule.notes: 10 min walk. Gold-leaf interiors + chirping floorboards.

schedule.activity: ⚔️ Samurai and Ninja Museum - armor + sword + shuriken
schedule.notes: Try on samurai armor, handle katana, throw shuriken!

schedule.activity: Dinner - Pontocho kawadoko riverside terrace
schedule.notes: Only available May-September. Perfect timing!

schedule.activity: Kamo River evening walk - sit on the stone steps
schedule.notes: Classic Kyoto evening moment.

schedule.activity: Dormy Inn Kyoto onsen

tips: Nijo nightingale floors: walk as slowly and quietly as possible
Pontocho kawadoko terrace is only May-September - don't miss it

itinerary.label: Fushimi Inari + Kinkaku-ji + Farewell Gion
itinerary.last_night_note: Last night in Kyoto - pack for Osaka tonight!

schedule.activity: Taxi to Inari Station
⛩️ Fushimi Inari - climb to Yotsutsuji + beyond!
schedule.notes: Go PAST Yotsutsuji - most tourists miss the best upper section. Allow 1.5+ hrs.

schedule.activity: JR Nara Line > Kyoto Station
schedule.notes: 5 min.

schedule.activity: Nishiki Market - 130 stalls, graze everything!
schedule.notes: Arrive hungry!

schedule.activity: 🥇 Kinkaku-ji - the Golden Pavilion
schedule.notes: Taxi. Gold leaf shimmers in afternoon light - 14:45 timing is perfect.

schedule.activity: Sekkatei teahouse matcha + exit matcha soft serve
Taxi to Gion area
Gion antique shopping - Shinmonzen + Furumonzen
schedule.notes: Genuine world-class Kyoyaki ceramics.

schedule.activity: Maruyama Park - weeping cherry + locals picnicking
Yasaka Shrine at dusk - lanterns glowing
Hanamikoji Street - farewell Gion walk
FAREWELL KYOTO DINNER - Pontocho kawadoko
schedule.notes: Last night in Kyoto - make it special.

schedule.activity: Final Kamo River walk - say goodbye to Kyoto
Dormy Inn onsen - final Kyoto soak
schedule.notes: Pack bags for Osaka tonight!

tips: Fushimi Inari: go beyond Yotsutsuji - the crowds thin out and the views open up
Pack bags tonight for early Osaka departure tomorrow

itinerary.label: Osaka Castle + Kuromon + Dotonbori

schedule.activity: Check out Dormy Inn Kyoto Gion
Taxi to Kyoto Station
Hikari Shinkansen Kyoto > Shin-Osaka
schedule.notes: 15 min! Shortest Shinkansen of the trip. JR Pass.

schedule.activity: Taxi to Dormy Inn Namba ANNEX - drop bags
Taxi to Osaka Castle
Osaka Castle outer grounds - free walk
schedule.notes: Giant stone walls, moat, volunteer English guides!

schedule.activity: Lunch in castle park
🏯 Osaka Castle main tower - 8 floors + observation deck
schedule.notes: Museum + 360 degree views + gold tiger roof!

schedule.activity: JR Osaka Loop Line > Namba
schedule.notes: 20 min. JR Pass.

schedule.activity: Check in Dormy Inn Namba ANNEX
schedule.notes: Lawson convenience store attached!

schedule.activity: Kuromon Ichiba Market - Osaka's Kitchen!
schedule.notes: 170 stalls. Graze everything.

schedule.activity: Amerikamura - Triangle Park + vintage + records
Shinsaibashi Shopping Arcade + Don Quijote
Dotonbori at dusk - neon canal reflections!
schedule.notes: Stand on Ebisu Bridge for full neon canal view.

schedule.activity: Glico Running Man - mandatory couple photo!
schedule.notes: Ebisu Bridge by the canal.

schedule.activity: Takoyaki at Ichifuji - the original and most authentic
schedule.notes: Not the flashiest stall but the best flavor.

schedule.activity: Dinner - Kani Doraku (giant mechanical crab!)
schedule.notes: King crab course meal by the canal.

schedule.activity: Bar hopping - Bar K whisky + Craft Beer Base Kamikaze
♨️ ONSEN 5 - Dormy Inn rooftop natural hot spring
schedule.notes: Natural hot spring + city views. Open til midnight!

schedule.activity: Free late night ramen - Dormy Inn!
schedule.notes: Served 21:30-23:00 nightly. Do NOT miss.

tips: Dotonbori: stand on Ebisu Bridge at dusk facing west for the classic neon shot
Dormy Inn free ramen: served 21:30-23:00 nightly - do not miss it

itinerary.label: Den Den Town + Wide View Hida
itinerary.luggage_note: Send large bags via TA-Q-BIN to Residence Hotel Takayama this morning. Bags arrive May 22.

schedule.activity: TA-Q-BIN bags to Residence Hotel Takayama Station
schedule.notes: Hotel front desk. Address: 6-105 Hanasatomachi, Takayama.

schedule.activity: Check out Dormy Inn Namba - daypack only
Breakfast - Lawson attached to hotel
Den Den Town (Nipponbashi) - Osaka's Akihabara
schedule.notes: 10 min walk. Joshin + Rocket retro games + Animate + Figure King.

schedule.activity: Walk to Shinsekai district
schedule.notes: 15 min walk.

schedule.activity: Shinsekai street level - Jan Jan Yokocho retro arcade
Fugu (puffer fish) breakfast at standing restaurant
schedule.notes: Licensed chefs - fully safe!

schedule.activity: Subway to Osaka Station
GREEN CAR Hikari Shinkansen Osaka > Nagoya - reserve at station
schedule.notes: 50 min. JR Pass.

schedule.activity: Quick lunch - Nagoya ekiben (miso katsu bento!)
schedule.notes: Platform shops at Nagoya.

schedule.activity: Wide View Hida Limited Express Nagoya > Takayama
schedule.notes: LEFT side A/B seats! Hida River gorge ~90 min in. JR Pass.

schedule.activity: Arrive Takayama Station
schedule.notes: 🏔️ Welcome to the Japanese Alps!

schedule.activity: Check in Residence Hotel - 30 SECONDS from station!
schedule.notes: Full apartment with kitchen. Night 1 of 2.

schedule.activity: Sanmachi Suji historic district + sake tastings
schedule.notes: 10 min walk. Look for sugidama (cedar balls) above brewery entrances.

schedule.activity: Takayama Jinya - only surviving Edo government house in Japan
Higashiyama Temple Walk - hilltop views
Dinner - Hida beef ramen or sukiyaki
Sanmachi Suji at night - lanterns and silence
schedule.notes: One of Japan's most beautiful quiet experiences.

Sanmachi Suji at night is one of Japan's most beautiful quiet experiences - don't skip it

itinerary.label: Morning Market + Hida Folk Village
itinerary.last_night_note: Last night in Takayama - pack for Tokyo tomorrow!
itinerary.luggage_note: Large bags arrive from Osaka today! Collect at front desk.

schedule.activity: Breakfast - Lawson near Takayama Station
Miyagawa Morning Market - running since Edo period
schedule.notes: 5 min walk. Bring CASH - most stalls cash only!

schedule.activity: Collect large bags at hotel (arrived via TA-Q-BIN)
schedule.notes: Hotel front desk.

schedule.activity: Morning Sanmachi Suji - mist + quiet lanes
Kusakabe Folk Museum - 1879 preserved merchant house
Cedar Avenue (Sugi-namiki) - 360 year old cryptomeria trees
schedule.notes: 15 min walk. Almost no tourists!

schedule.activity: Lunch at Amazake-chaya - 400yr old thatched teahouse
schedule.notes: Open since 1638!

schedule.activity: Taxi to Hida Folk Village
🏡 Hida Folk Village - 30+ gassho-zukuri farmhouses
schedule.notes: Walk inside every open farmhouse! Allow 1.5-2 hrs.

schedule.activity: Taxi back to Sanmachi Suji
Final Sanmachi - sake shopping + crafts + Hida beef manju
Dinner - Hida beef sukiyaki + hoba miso + local sake
Final Takayama night walk - lanterns + total silence
schedule.notes: Unforgettable.

schedule.activity: Back to hotel - pack for Tokyo tomorrow
schedule.notes: Set alarm for 6:50 AM.

tips: Morning Market: 7 AM sharp for freshest produce. Bring cash - cash only stalls.
Buy sake DIRECT from breweries - namazake (unpasteurized) unavailable outside Hida

itinerary.label: Morning Market > Wide View Hida > SUZUKA CIRCUIT > Tokyo

schedule.activity: Breakfast - convenience store near Takayama Station
Miyagawa Morning Market - final farewell
schedule.notes: 5 min walk. Different vendors daily.

schedule.activity: Check out Residence Hotel
schedule.notes: 30 SECOND walk to station!

schedule.activity: Wide View Hida - Takayama > Nagoya

schedule.activity: Arrive Nagoya Station
Quick lunch - Hitsumabushi eel or miso katsu at platform shops
schedule.notes: Eat fast!

schedule.activity: Buy Kintetsu Limited Express tickets - Nagoya to Shiroko AND return
schedule.notes: Kintetsu office EAST SIDE of Nagoya Station. NOT JR Pass. ~Y1,720 each way. Buy BOTH directions now.

schedule.activity: Kintetsu Limited Express - Nagoya > Shiroko
schedule.notes: 50 min. Reserved reclining seat.

schedule.activity: Arrive Shiroko Station - follow English signs to shuttle bus
Mie Kotsu shuttle bus > Suzuka Circuit
schedule.notes: 20 min. ~Y500. Suica accepted.

schedule.activity: 🏁 Arrive Suzuka Circuit! Buy Race Day Special Passport + Pit Walk Pass
schedule.notes: Buy at main gate on arrival.

schedule.activity: Explore circuit grounds - Motopia Park + Racing Theater + Ferris Wheel
schedule.notes: Racing Theater has F1 car displays. Ferris Wheel gives aerial track view.

schedule.activity: 🏎️ PIT WALK - walk the actual Suzuka pitlane!
schedule.notes: No umbrellas in pitlane - use rainwear if raining.

schedule.activity: Super Formula Rounds 4 & 5 - qualifying or race session
schedule.notes: Japan's premier single-seater. Same Suzuka corners as F1 - 130R, Spoon, Degner.

schedule.activity: Head to shuttle bus - leave 15 min before session ends if possible
schedule.notes: Buses get very crowded right after sessions.

schedule.activity: Shuttle bus > Shiroko Station
schedule.notes: 20 min.

schedule.activity: Kintetsu Limited Express - Shiroko > Nagoya
schedule.notes: 50 min. Use pre-bought return ticket.

schedule.activity: Arrive Nagoya Station - board evening Shinkansen
Hikari Shinkansen - Nagoya > Tokyo
schedule.notes: ~1 hr 40 min. Ordinary reserved. JR Pass.

schedule.activity: Arrive Tokyo Station
JR Chuo Line > Shinjuku
schedule.notes: 30 min. JR Pass.

schedule.activity: Check in Yuen Shinjuku - night 1 of 2
schedule.notes: 8 min walk from Shinjuku Station. Yukata provided!

schedule.activity: Late dinner - Tsunahachi tempura or Omoide Yokocho
schedule.notes: Both 5 min walk from hotel.

schedule.activity: ♨️ ONSEN 6 - Yuen Shinjuku rooftop onsen
schedule.notes: Indoor + outdoor with Shinjuku skyline!

tips: Kintetsu is NOT JR Pass - buy at Kintetsu office EAST side Nagoya Station
Buy BOTH outbound and return Kintetsu tickets at the same time
Pit Walk: no umbrellas allowed in the pitlane - pack a poncho

itinerary.label: A-PIT JDM + Harajuku + Shopping

schedule.activity: Breakfast at Yuen Shinjuku
Check out - store bags at hotel front desk
Rinkai Line from Shinjuku > Shinonome (via Osaki)
schedule.notes: 35 min.

schedule.activity: 🚗 A-PIT Super Autobacs - JDM paradise! 4 floors
schedule.notes: Walk the PARKING LOT first - show cars change regularly. 3 min walk from Shinonome station.

schedule.activity: Rinkai Line + JR Yamanote > Harajuku
schedule.notes: 35 min.

schedule.activity: Lunch in Harajuku or Omotesando area
Cat Street - Off-White, Supreme Japan, BAPE, Neighborhood
schedule.notes: Japan-only releases not in US stores!

schedule.activity: Ura-Harajuku boutiques - Sacai, Kapital, Nanamica, Beams Japan
JR Yamanote > Shinjuku
schedule.notes: 7 min.

schedule.activity: Isetan Shinjuku - B1 food hall + craft floor + menswear
Tokyu Hands + Don Quijote Shinjuku
Collect bags from Yuen Shinjuku
schedule.notes: Check back in for second night.

schedule.activity: Dinner - New York Bar Park Hyatt or Kozue
schedule.notes: Optional splurge. Lost in Translation vibes.

schedule.activity: Omoide Yokocho final visit
schedule.notes: 2 min from hotel.

schedule.activity: Yuen Shinjuku rooftop onsen - last onsen of the trip
schedule.notes: Savour it.

tips: A-PIT parking lot first before going inside - the show cars are the highlight
Cat Street: Supreme Japan has Japan-only releases different from US stores
Ask every store for tax-free shopping - show passport for 10% refund on Y5,000+ purchases

itinerary.label: Takashimaya + Final Shopping + Depart

schedule.activity: Wake up - final morning in Japan
schedule.notes: Last breakfast in Japan!

schedule.activity: Breakfast at Yuen Shinjuku
Check out Yuen Shinjuku - store bags at front desk
Walk to Takashimaya Times Square
schedule.notes: 8 min walk from hotel.

schedule.activity: Takashimaya B1-B2 food hall - Kit Kats, wagashi, sake, matcha, yuzu
schedule.notes: Best souvenir shopping in Tokyo.

schedule.activity: Takashimaya 6F craft floor - ceramics, lacquerware, washi, knives
Takashimaya 8F tax-free counter - 10% refund, bring passport!
schedule.notes: Consolidate ALL purchases here.

schedule.activity: Shinjuku final shopping - Beams Japan + Tokyu Hands
Final Tokyo lunch - Afuri Ramen or Tsunahachi tempura
Collect bags from Yuen Shinjuku
schedule.notes: 8 min walk.

schedule.activity: N'EX Narita Express - Shinjuku > Narita Airport
schedule.notes: 90 min. JR Pass. DEPART ON TIME - tight connection at Narita.

schedule.activity: Arrive Narita Airport Terminal 1
schedule.notes: Go straight to Air Canada check-in.

schedule.activity: Check in AC 4 + bag drop
schedule.notes: Counter closes before 16:30. Move quickly.

schedule.activity: Security + immigration - Narita T1
schedule.notes: Allow 20-25 min.

schedule.activity: At gate - last Japan duty free!
schedule.notes: Yamazaki, Hibiki, Nikka whisky. Best prices in Japan.

schedule.activity: ✈️ AC 4 departs Tokyo Narita (NRT)
schedule.notes: Boeing 787-9. 8h 45m. Wi-Fi. Meal + Breakfast included.

schedule.activity: Land Vancouver (YVR) - same calendar day!
schedule.notes: You crossed the Date Line going east - gain a day back.

schedule.activity: ✈️ AC 1762 departs Vancouver (YVR)
schedule.notes: Boeing 737 MAX 8. 4h 27m.

schedule.activity: 🏠 Land Austin-Bergstrom Airport (AUS)
schedule.notes: Welcome home! Uber back to Cedar Park ~1 hour.

tips: N'EX at 14:20 is non-negotiable - missing it means missing the flight
Return Suica cards at Narita JR office for remaining balance + Y500 deposit
Whisky: Narita duty free usually has Yamazaki and Hibiki even when shops are sold out

bookings.alert: TICKETS ON SALE: Saturday April 11 at 9:00 PM Austin, TX time. Buy Race Day Special Passport for May 23 + Pit Walk Pass.
bookings.sale_date_austin: Saturday April 11, 2026 at 9:00 PM Austin, TX Time (CDT)

bookings.where: Purchase before leaving Austin - cannot buy in Japan

bookings.sale_date_austin: Friday April 3, 2026 at 10:01 AM Austin, TX Time (CDT)

bookings.item: Gran Class seats (May 17) - Odawara Station JR office on arrival morning
Green Car seats - Osaka to Nagoya (May 21)
bookings.where: JR Midori-no-madoguchi at Shin-Osaka or Osaka Station

experiences.item: Kimono dress-up at Wargo Asakusa
Sumo Natsu Basho - Makuuchi top division bouts
Super Formula live racing + pit walk at Suzuka Circuit
JDM A-PIT Super Autobacs - 4 floors
teamLab Planets immersive digital art
Chureito Pagoda - iconic Mt Fuji view
Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway + ring the love bell
Odakyu Romance Car scenic train ride
Gran Class Shinkansen - Odawara to Kyoto
Fujiya Hotel mineral onsen x3 (evenings + dawn)
Tenzan Tohji-kyo forest public onsen
Authentic matcha tea ceremony 90 min at Camellia
Samurai Museum - armor fitting + sword + shuriken
Fushimi Inari thousand torii gates
Kinkaku-ji the Golden Pavilion
Gion Hanamikoji Street and spotting maiko
Osaka Castle 8 floors + observation deck
Dotonbori neon canal + Glico Man couple photo
Sanmachi Suji Takayama historic lanes + sake tastings
Hida Folk Village gassho-zukuri thatched farmhouses
Harajuku Cat Street - Supreme Japan, BAPE, Off-White
Takashimaya Times Square food hall + craft floor
Narita duty free Japanese whisky

documents.item: JR Pass vouchers x2 - in carry-on ONLY, never checked bag
Air Canada booking confirmation (AC 1763 + AC 3 / AC 4 + AC 1762)
Suzuka Circuit Race Day Special Passport + Pit Walk Pass

essentials.item: Portable WiFi / pocket WiFi (pre-book for Japan)
Universal power adapter (Japan uses Type A - same plug as US)
Cash in USD to exchange on arrival
Comfortable walking shoes (10,000+ steps per day)
Slip-on shoes for May 13 (kimono + teamLab water rooms)
Small daypack for TA-Q-BIN forwarding days (May 15 + May 21)
Small towel for onsen (Fujiya + Dormy Inn provide towels)

clothing.item: Smart casual for Gran Class train and Fujiya Hotel dining
Easy-remove shoes for temples, tea ceremony, tatami rooms

tech.item: Camera + extra batteries and memory cards
Download Japan maps offline in Google Maps (do on home WiFi)
Download Japanese language pack in Google Translate (do on home WiFi)

transport_tips: JR Pass covers all Shinkansen except Nozomi and Mizuho - always book Hikari or Kodama
Kintetsu to Suzuka is NOT JR Pass - buy separately at Kintetsu office east side Nagoya Station
Suica card works on all non-JR metro lines, buses, and even convenience stores and vending machines
IC card readers everywhere - tap in and tap out
Last train typically around midnight in major cities - do not miss it
etiquette_tips: No eating or drinking while walking (except at food stalls and festivals)
Trash cans are rare - carry a small bag for waste
Quiet on trains - no phone calls, keep music in headphones
Onsen rules: shower before entering, no swimwear, tattoos may be restricted at some
Cash is still widely used - always carry some yen
Remove shoes when entering temples, traditional restaurants, and some museums

apps_to_download.name: Google Maps
apps_to_download.purpose: Navigation + download Japan offline maps before leaving

apps_to_download.name: Google Translate
apps_to_download.purpose: Camera translate menus and signs (download Japanese offline)
